clusters.title: Trump tells Congress ceasefire means he does not need their approval for Iran war
clusters.neutral_headline: As Iran war hits key 60-day deadline, Congress and Trump face choices
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, CBS News, NBC World and others

items.title: Trump tells Congress ceasefire means he does not need their approval for Iran war
items.standfirst: The president writes that hostilities "have terminated" because of the ceasefire, arguing he does not need congressional authorisation.
items.content: The president writes that hostilities "have terminated" because of the ceasefire, arguing he does not need congressional authorisation.

items.title: Trump tells Congress "hostilities" with Iran have "terminated"
items.standfirst: President Trump on Friday told Congress that hostilities with Iran have "terminated," addressing a critical 60-day deadline.
items.content: President Trump on Friday told Congress that hostilities with Iran have "terminated," addressing a critical 60-day deadline.

items.title: Trump says he doesn't need congressional authorization for Iran war, citing ceasefire
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump on Friday sent letters to Congress explaining that, due to the ceasefire, he doesn't need its authorization for military operations in Iran, even though the conflict hit the 60-day mark this week."On April 7, 2026, I ordered a two-week ceasefire.
items.content: President Donald Trump on Friday sent letters to Congress explaining that, due to the ceasefire, he doesn't need its authorization for military operations in Iran, even though the conflict hit the 60-day mark this week."On April 7, 2026, I ordered a two-week ceasefire.

items.title: As Iran war hits key 60-day deadline, Congress and Trump face choices
items.standfirst: The War Powers Resolution sets deadlines for the president to end hostilities without congressional approval.
items.content: The War Powers Resolution sets deadlines for the president to end hostilities without congressional approval.

items.title: 60-day deadline for Congress' Iran war extension approval
items.standfirst: In the United States, lawmakers remain deadlocked over the War Powers Resolution, which requires Congress to approve continued military action or withdraw troops once hostilities reach the 60-day mark. With the deadline approaching and no agreement in sight, a decision remains out of reach. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle shares his insights.
items.content: In the United States, lawmakers remain deadlocked over the War Powers Resolution, which requires Congress to approve continued military action or withdraw troops once hostilities reach the 60-day mark. With the deadline approaching and no agreement in sight, a decision remains out of reach. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle shares his insights.

items.title: US administration says war "terminated", no approval needed
items.standfirst: In the United States, lawmakers remain deadlocked over the War Powers Resolution, which requires Congress to approve continued military action or withdraw troops once hostilities reach the 60-day mark. With the deadline approaching and no agreement in sight, a decision remains out of reach. France24 International Affairs Commentator Douglas Herbert shares his insights.
items.content: In the United States, lawmakers remain deadlocked over the War Powers Resolution, which requires Congress to approve continued military action or withdraw troops once hostilities reach the 60-day mark. With the deadline approaching and no agreement in sight, a decision remains out of reach. France24 International Affairs Commentator Douglas Herbert shares his insights.

items.title: Trump administration says hostilities in Iran ‘terminated’ ahead of war powers deadline
items.standfirst: US president faced a 60-day deadline on Friday to end the Iran war or make the case to Congress for extending itA US-Iran ceasefire that began in early April has “terminated” hostilities between the two sides for the purposes of an approaching congressional war powers deadline, a senior official of the Trump administration said on Thursday.Donald Trump faced a deadline on Friday to end the Iran war or make the case to Congress for extending it, but the date was most likely to pass without altering the course of the war. Continue reading...
items.content: US president faced a 60-day deadline on Friday to end the Iran war or make the case to Congress for extending itA US-Iran ceasefire that began in early April has “terminated” hostilities between the two sides for the purposes of an approaching congressional war powers deadline, a senior official of the Trump administration said on Thursday.Donald Trump faced a deadline on Friday to end the Iran war or make the case to Congress for extending it, but the date was most likely to pass without altering the course of the war. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Trump tears up part of EU tariff deal to raises import duties on cars and lorries
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump announces 25 percent tariffs on European Union cars, trucks
bullet_summary: The European Union's trade chair has hit back at Donald Trump after he announced plans to increase tariffs on vehicles exported to the US
US ​President Donald Trump on Friday said he will increase tariffs ​on ‌vehicles from the European Union to ‌25% next week, accusing the bloc of ⁠not complying with its 2025 trade deal

items.title: Trump tears up part of EU tariff deal to raises import duties on cars and lorries
items.standfirst: US president says tariff on vehicles imported from EU will rise to 25% and accuses bloc of non-complianceDonald Trump has said he is tearing up part of the tariff deal he struck with EU leaders at his golf course in Scotland last summer, criticising Brussels for taking so long to ratify the deal.Blindsiding Brussels late on Friday, a public holiday in much of Europe, he announced that he would be increasing tariffs on cars and lorries imported into the US from the EU from 15% to 25% from next week. Continue reading...
items.content: US president says tariff on vehicles imported from EU will rise to 25% and accuses bloc of non-complianceDonald Trump has said he is tearing up part of the tariff deal he struck with EU leaders at his golf course in Scotland last summer, criticising Brussels for taking so long to ratify the deal.Blindsiding Brussels late on Friday, a public holiday in much of Europe, he announced that he would be increasing tariffs on cars and lorries imported into the US from the EU from 15% to 25% from next week. Continue reading...

items.content: US president says EU ‘not complying’ with current trade deal, launches new tariffs to begin next week.

items.title: EU hits back at 'unacceptable' Donald Trump over plans to increase tariffs
items.standfirst: The European Union's trade chair has hit back at Donald Trump after he announced plans to increase tariffs on vehicles exported to the US.
items.content: The European Union's trade chair has hit back at Donald Trump after he announced plans to increase tariffs on vehicles exported to the US.

items.title: Trump says he will raise tariffs on EU autos to 25% for 'not complying' with trade deal
items.standfirst: US ​President Donald Trump on Friday said he will increase tariffs ​on ‌vehicles from the European Union to ‌25% next week, accusing the bloc of ⁠not complying with its 2025 trade deal.
items.content: US ​President Donald Trump on Friday said he will increase tariffs ​on ‌vehicles from the European Union to ‌25% next week, accusing the bloc of ⁠not complying with its 2025 trade deal.

clusters.title: Trump says Iran seeks terms he ‘can’t agree to’ in latest peace proposal

bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, CBS News, Guardian World and others

items.title: Trump says Iran seeks terms he ‘can’t agree to’ in latest peace proposal
items.standfirst: Trump says the Iranian offer sent via Pakistan contains terms he's not satisfied with.
items.content: Trump says the Iranian offer sent via Pakistan contains terms he's not satisfied with.

items.title: Trump says he's "not satisfied" after Iran sends new proposal for talks
items.standfirst: President Trump took questions before departing the White House for an event in Florida. The remarks come after Iran submitted, through Pakistani mediators, a proposal for a second round of peace talks. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi reports.
items.content: President Trump took questions before departing the White House for an event in Florida. The remarks come after Iran submitted, through Pakistani mediators, a proposal for a second round of peace talks. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi reports.

items.title: Iran offers new peace proposal to US but Trump ‘not satisfied’
items.standfirst: Tehran reportedly passed proposal to mediators in Pakistan on Thursday night, though its contents are not yet clearIran has passed a new proposal to Pakistani mediators in the latest effort to end the war with the US, but Donald Trump said he was not “satisfied” by it.“Right now, we have talks going on, they’re not getting there,” he told reporters, adding that his options remained “either blast them away or make a deal”. Continue reading...
items.content: Tehran reportedly passed proposal to mediators in Pakistan on Thursday night, though its contents are not yet clearIran has passed a new proposal to Pakistani mediators in the latest effort to end the war with the US, but Donald Trump said he was not “satisfied” by it.“Right now, we have talks going on, they’re not getting there,” he told reporters, adding that his options remained “either blast them away or make a deal”. Continue reading...

items.title: 🔴 Middle East war live: Iran has sent its latest proposal to US via Pakistani mediators

clusters.title: Trump threatens to withdraw troops from Italy and Spain
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump threatens to withdraw troops from Italy and Spain
bullet_summary: Donald Trump has threatened to remove US troops from Germany after the country's chancellor said America was being "humiliated" by Iran
Reported by Guardian World, Al Jazeera, Sky News World

items.title: Trump threatens to withdraw troops from Italy and Spain
items.standfirst: US president says European countries are ‘absolutely horrible’ to refuse to support operations in strait of HormuzDonald Trump has threatened to withdraw US troops from Italy and Spain, a day after saying he was looking at reducing the number deployed in Germany.The US president’s threat to Germany came after its chancellor, Friedrich Merz, said America was being “humiliated” by Iran, and follows weeks of criticism by Trump of Nato allies for not helping to reopen the strait of Hormuz. Continue reading...
items.content: US president says European countries are ‘absolutely horrible’ to refuse to support operations in strait of HormuzDonald Trump has threatened to withdraw US troops from Italy and Spain, a day after saying he was looking at reducing the number deployed in Germany.The US president’s threat to Germany came after its chancellor, Friedrich Merz, said America was being “humiliated” by Iran, and follows weeks of criticism by Trump of Nato allies for not helping to reopen the strait of Hormuz. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump’s threat: Why cutting US troops in Europe won’t be easy
items.standfirst: Amid transatlantic tensions over the Iran war, Trump has threatened to reduce troops in Germany.
items.content: Amid transatlantic tensions over the Iran war, Trump has threatened to reduce troops in Germany.

items.title: Trump threatens to withdraw troops from Germany
items.standfirst: Donald Trump has threatened to remove US troops from Germany after the country's chancellor said America was being "humiliated" by Iran.
items.content: Donald Trump has threatened to remove US troops from Germany after the country's chancellor said America was being "humiliated" by Iran.

clusters.title: Congress reduces Bolsonaro's jair term
clusters.neutral_headline: Brazil's Congress approves plan to drastically cut Bolsonaro's jail term
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World, Guardian World

items.title: Congress reduces Bolsonaro's jair term
items.standfirst: Brazil’s Congress has voted to reduce the 27-year prison sentence of former President Jair Bolsonaro, though the highly controversial decision is expected to be appealed. Bolsonaro was convicted of plotting a coup after losing the 2022 presidential election, and the move to shorten his sentence is widely seen as a setback for current President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, as reported by Liza Kaminov.
items.content: Brazil’s Congress has voted to reduce the 27-year prison sentence of former President Jair Bolsonaro, though the highly controversial decision is expected to be appealed. Bolsonaro was convicted of plotting a coup after losing the 2022 presidential election, and the move to shorten his sentence is widely seen as a setback for current President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, as reported by Liza Kaminov.

items.title: Brazil's Congress approves plan to drastically cut Bolsonaro's jail term
items.standfirst: Last year ex-president Jair Bolsonaro was sentenced to 27 years in jail for plotting a coup after losing an election.
items.content: Last year ex-president Jair Bolsonaro was sentenced to 27 years in jail for plotting a coup after losing an election.

items.title: Brazil’s congress approves bill reducing prison sentence of former president Jair Bolsonaro
items.standfirst: President Lula’s veto of the bill was overturned by Brazil’s congress and senate, meaning it now awaits confirmation by supreme court Brazil’s largely conservative congress has approved a bill reducing the prison sentence of the far-right former president Jair Bolsonaro, who was convicted last year of attempting a coup.The bill had initially been passed by congress in December, but President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva vetoed it in January in a symbolic move marking three years since Bolsonaro supporters ransacked the capital, Brasília. Continue reading...
items.content: President Lula’s veto of the bill was overturned by Brazil’s congress and senate, meaning it now awaits confirmation by supreme court Brazil’s largely conservative congress has approved a bill reducing the prison sentence of the far-right former president Jair Bolsonaro, who was convicted last year of attempting a coup.The bill had initially been passed by congress in December, but President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva vetoed it in January in a symbolic move marking three years since Bolsonaro supporters ransacked the capital, Brasília. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Sabastian Sawe receives hero’s welcome in Kenya after sub-two hour marathon feat
clusters.neutral_headline: Sabastian Sawe receives hero’s welcome in Kenya after sub-two hour marathon feat
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: Sabastian Sawe receives hero’s welcome in Kenya after sub-two hour marathon feat
items.standfirst: Record-breaker says London Marathon win was ‘a victory for all of us’ as he is greeted by family and friends in EldoretHugged, cheered and adorned with garlands, the first man to run an official marathon in under two hours has returned as a hero to his home village in Kenya.Sabastian Sawe, who stunned the world when he clocked 1h 59m 30s in the London Marathon last weekend, flew in a Kenyan military plane normally reserved for special operations on Thursday to his home region of western Kenya. Continue reading...
items.content: Record-breaker says London Marathon win was ‘a victory for all of us’ as he is greeted by family and friends in EldoretHugged, cheered and adorned with garlands, the first man to run an official marathon in under two hours has returned as a hero to his home village in Kenya.Sabastian Sawe, who stunned the world when he clocked 1h 59m 30s in the London Marathon last weekend, flew in a Kenyan military plane normally reserved for special operations on Thursday to his home region of western Kenya. Continue reading...

items.standfirst: BBC reports from Kenya, home to Sabastian Sawe, the first man to run a marathon in under two hours.
items.content: BBC reports from Kenya, home to Sabastian Sawe, the first man to run a marathon in under two hours.

items.title: Woman charged over death of two eight-year-old girls after Wimbledon car crash
items.standfirst: Claire Freemantle accused of causing death and serious injury by dangerous driving when vehicle hit school in 2023The driver of a car that crashed into a south London primary school has been charged with causing death by dangerous driving after two eight-year-old girls were killed.Claire Freemantle is accused of two counts of causing death by dangerous driving and seven counts of causing serious injury by dangerous driving after the incident at The Study Prep school in Wimbledon in July 2023. Continue reading...
items.content: Claire Freemantle accused of causing death and serious injury by dangerous driving when vehicle hit school in 2023The driver of a car that crashed into a south London primary school has been charged with causing death by dangerous driving after two eight-year-old girls were killed.Claire Freemantle is accused of two counts of causing death by dangerous driving and seven counts of causing serious injury by dangerous driving after the incident at The Study Prep school in Wimbledon in July 2023. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Aung San Suu Kyi moved to house arrest
clusters.neutral_headline: Myanmar ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi moved to house arrest, military says

items.title: Aung San Suu Kyi moved to house arrest
items.standfirst: This world tour now takes us to Myanmar, where Aung San Suu Kyi will serve the remainder of her sentence under house arrest, following a decision by the head of the ruling junta, now acting as president. The Nobel Peace Prize laureate had until now been held in an undisclosed location.
items.content: This world tour now takes us to Myanmar, where Aung San Suu Kyi will serve the remainder of her sentence under house arrest, following a decision by the head of the ruling junta, now acting as president. The Nobel Peace Prize laureate had until now been held in an undisclosed location.

items.content: The Nobel Peace Prize laureate has been in detention since she was ousted in a military coup in 2021.

clusters.title: Passengers pulled from water after learner driver crashes bus into major European river

items.title: Passengers pulled from water after learner driver crashes bus into major European river
items.standfirst: Four people have been rescued from the River Seine in Paris after a trainee bus driver crashed through a barrier and into the water.
items.content: Four people have been rescued from the River Seine in Paris after a trainee bus driver crashed through a barrier and into the water.

items.title: Trainee driver crashes bus into River Seine
items.standfirst: The driver hit a parked car and veered off the road into the river - about 12 miles south of Paris - early on Thursday.
items.content: The driver hit a parked car and veered off the road into the river - about 12 miles south of Paris - early on Thursday.

clusters.title: Israel intercepts Gaza flotilla near Crete and detains 175 activists
clusters.neutral_headline: Israel intercepts Gaza flotilla near Crete and detains 175 activists
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Guardian World

items.title: Israel intercepts Gaza flotilla near Crete and detains 175 activists
items.standfirst: Pro-Palestinian activists say 22 boats carrying aid for Gaza were illegally intercepted in international waters near the Greek island.
items.content: Pro-Palestinian activists say 22 boats carrying aid for Gaza were illegally intercepted in international waters near the Greek island.

items.title: Israel intercepts and detains crews of Gaza aid flotilla near Crete
items.standfirst: Global Sumud Flotilla describes interception as ‘violent raid’ while Turkey condemns it as ‘act of piracy’Israeli forces have intercepted and detained the crews of at least 22 boats near the Greek island of Crete from a flotilla that is attempting to break Israel’s maritime blockade of the Gaza Strip to deliver humanitarian aid.The Global Sumud Flotilla, consisting of about 58 vessels carrying people from across 70 countries, departed from Italy on Sunday. Continue reading...
